title: Signal
description: One claim, from one source, about one subject. Append-only. Signals are never rewritten to agree with each other — conflicting signals coexist by design, and the resolved record in data/sites/ carries the conflict openly. Written by gn_info_scout; the founding corpus was hand-carried (platform ADR-0046) and says so in `collected_by`.

source.description: id from data/sources.yml. Referential integrity is CI-enforced.

url.description: The exact page the claim was read on — not the site root, not a search result.

subject_type.description: pack resolves into data/sites/; maker resolves into data/operators.yml.

subject_id.description: The resolved subject's id, when known. Omit it and set site_hint when the subject is new.

site_hint.description: Free-text identification for a subject that has no record yet: '<maker as reported> <model> — <capacity as reported>'.

field.description: The attribute claimed, from the profile's attribute vocabulary (capacity_l, weight_g, price, acquired_date, ...).

value.description: What the source says, as the source says it. Units belong in the field name, not the value.

collected_by.description: Who read it: a gnome receipt stamp (gn_info_scout/1.0.1) or a console session (console:EPIC4-05). Never left blank — a claim's collector is part of its provenance.

confidence.description: high = the maker or acquirer speaking for itself. medium = reputable press or review. low = anything else. Absent means unrated, not high.

conflicts_with.description: Signal ids this claim contradicts. Two signals may disagree and BOTH be accurate reports of what their sources said — including two signals from the same URL.
